title: Pellionia minutiflora
intro: Pellionia minutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. Traditionally, Pellionia minutiflora has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pellionia minutiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pellionia minutiflora is often used to make herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pellionia minutiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It belongs to the genus Pellionia, which is a group of plants commonly known as the net-veined plants. The Urticaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of vines, shrubs, and trees. Pellionia minutiflora is native to tropical regions of Asia and is often found growing in shaded areas. The plant has small, white flowers and is often used in horticulture as an ornamental species.
